Dashboard Archive: 2026-03-20 Home
About This Dashboard
What It Does
Short-term momentum ranker scoring 700+ tickers on returns (1d to 1y), SMA gate (above 30/50/100/200 day), ratio consistency across timeframes, and resilience on bad SPY days. Higher composite scores indicate stronger, more consistent short-term momentum.
Today's Reading
Today's momentum-ranker data shows two extreme performers at the top, with TYRA and TNGX dominating the rankings. TYRA scores 97.6 (rank 1) with exceptional consistency—it's up 246% over one year, passes all five ratio tests, clears all four SMA gates, and maintains strong outperformance versus SPY (+231%), QQQ (+224%), and IAU (+196%) over the same period. TNGX is even more dramatic on raw returns, posting a 1,009% one-year gain that dwarfs SPY, but shows instability in its week-to-day ratio (-15.59) and only passes 4 of 5 ratio tests, explaining why it doesn't rank as high as TYRA. The key difference is that TYRA demonstrates steady, compound momentum across all timeframes with a 0.5996 bad-SPY resilience score, while TNGX shows more erratic shorter-term behavior despite its astronomical long-term gains. Both securities are trading above all major moving averages, suggesting they've been in sustained uptrends, though TYRA's smoother ratio progression and resilience scoring indicate more reliable near-term momentum mechanics.
For educational and informational purposes only. Not financial advice. Past performance does not guarantee future results.
